evidence: Lifting.lean:154, docstring explicitly frames it as analytic continuation along homotopic paths giving path-independent results (the classical monodromy theorem); QID has enwiki sitelink 'Monodromy theorem'.

Contractible.lean:49, class ContractibleSpace X := Nonempty (X ≃ₕ Unit); currently only tagged with generic Q746083 'homotopy', missing the tighter Q230689 'contractible space' (desc: shrinks continuously to a point).

HSpaces.lean:65, class HSpace X with hmul/e satisfying unit laws up to homotopy; docstring cites Wikipedia H-spaces. Q1562471 desc: 'topological space with a multiplication that is associative and unital up to homotopy' matches exactly.

HomotopyGroup.lean:85, abbrev LoopSpace := Path x x (space of based loops, notation Ω); currently only tagged Q662830 'fundamental group' (the quotient group, not the space). Q2066070 desc: 'space of basepoint preserving maps from a circle'.

HomotopyGroup.lean:599-606, CommGroup instance for pi_n(n>=2) proved via EckmannHilton.commGroup; already tagged Q1626416 'homotopy group' but missing the specific proof-technique QID that IS the theorem's content.
HomotopyGroup.lean:552-556, establishes EckmannHilton.IsUnital hypothesis for auxGroup i, the setup step of the Eckmann-Hilton argument used to prove pi_n commutative; currently unmapped entirely.
HomotopyGroup.lean:557-565, applies EckmannHilton.mul to show two group structures on HomotopyGroup coincide, the core interchange step of the Eckmann-Hilton argument; currently unmapped.

Lifting.lean:299-309 (IsCoveringMap ns), lifts a homotopy I x A -> X through a covering map given a lift of the t=0 slice -- textbook homotopy lifting property; currently tagged only homotopy-group/homotopy QIDs, missing this exact-match concept.

Lifting.lean:331, relative-homotopy variant of liftHomotopy for covering maps (PreconnectedSpace A); currently unmapped. Same homotopy lifting property concept, restricted to homotopies fixed on a subspace.

Lifting.lean:127-136, continuity of a lift g of a homotopy f:I x A -> X through a separated local homeomorphism p:E->X; the general (non-covering-map) form of the homotopy lifting property. Currently unmapped.

HSpaces.lean:121, every topological monoid (MulOneClass + ContinuousMul) yields an HSpace structure via its own operation -- the canonical family of examples cited directly in the H-space Wikipedia article.
Contractible.lean:56-60, the identity map on a ContractibleSpace is null-homotopic -- a direct structural consequence of contractibility, same tightest-QID gap as ContractibleSpace itself.
Contractible.lean:62-73, ContractibleSpace Y <-> (id Y).Nullhomotopic -- the iff-characterization of contractibility itself, currently entirely unmapped despite being the file's headline result.

LocallyContractible.lean:83, docstring says 'classical local contractibility (null-homotopic inclusions)'. Q15832668 label matches exactly but the item has no description/enwiki sitelink -- verify manually before use.
LocallyContractible.lean:173-186, proves the file's strong SLC notion implies classical local contractibility; same thin-QID caveat as LocallyContractibleSpace (no description/sitelink on Q15832668).
